faqTitle: Questions We Get All the Time
faq1Q: Is this WEBP to PNG converter really free?
faq1A: Yes, completely. There's no free tier with hidden limits, no premium version, and no credit card required. We keep it free because we use it ourselves.
faq2Q: Will the PNG quality be as good as the original WEBP?
faq2A: Actually, PNG can look even sharper than WEBP in some cases because it uses lossless compression. You won't lose any pixel data during the conversion — what you see in the preview is exactly what you get.
faq3Q: Why would I convert WEBP to PNG?
faq3A: WEBP is great for web performance, but it's not universally supported. Older software, some design tools, and most print workflows expect PNG or JPG. Converting to PNG gives you the widest compatibility without sacrificing image quality.
faq4Q: How long are my files kept on the server?
faq4A: Your uploaded files and converted PNGs are automatically deleted within 60 minutes. Once you close the download page, they're gone — we don't keep copies.
faq5Q: Can I convert WEBP files with transparency to PNG?
faq5A: Absolutely. PNG supports full alpha transparency, so any transparent areas in your WEBP will be faithfully preserved in the output. This makes it ideal for logos, icons, and design assets.
seo1Title: WEBP vs PNG — Which Should You Use?
seo1P1: Google introduced the WEBP format back in 2010 to make the web faster. At the same size, a WEBP image typically looks better than a JPEG and loads quicker than a PNG. That's why you'll find WEBP everywhere on modern websites — it's the format browsers love.
seo1P2: The problem is that WEBP doesn't travel well. Try opening a WEBP in Photoshop (without a plugin), email it to a client, or drop it into a Word document — and you'll quickly run into issues. PNG is the universal format that works everywhere: design software, presentations, print shops, messaging apps. When you need your image to just work, PNG is the safe choice.
seo2Title: When You Actually Need to Convert WEBP to PNG
seo2P: You've probably landed here because something refused to open your WEBP file. That happens more than you'd think. Social media platforms, stock image sites, and website builders all export in WEBP now — but downstream tools haven't caught up. Our converter handles that gap quietly and quickly. Drop your file, get your PNG, move on with your day.
